summary:
Claim: More condensing force is required when using spherical alloys compared to lathe-cut alloys.  
Evidence: - Spherical alloys exhibit poor interparticulate cohesion and tend to form voids during condensation due to inefficient packing. - Their structure leads to unbalanced stress distribution and reduced bulk density without sufficient compressive force.  
Answer: B

Answer: B. Answer choice: B
